findings.description: The PDF uses a three-column layout for agenda items (time, topic with sub-bullets, presenter name) which is collapsed in the HTML into a single-column flow where presenter names are placed inline in parentheses rather than right-aligned as a distinct column.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, presenter names like '(Neelufa Sarwar)', '(Brian McCrodden)', etc. are right-aligned in a separate column. In the HTML, they appear inline with the topic text.

findings.description: Bullet points in the PDF are rendered as dashes in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF uses bullet characters (•) for sub-items under each agenda topic. HTML uses dashes (-) instead.

findings.description: Bullet points converted from bullet symbols to dashes
findings.evidence: PDF uses bullet points (•) for sub-items under each agenda topic. HTML uses dashes (-) instead. This is a minor formatting change that does not affect meaning.

findings.description: Agenda items rendered as plain paragraphs rather than structured headings or a table
findings.evidence: The PDF presents the agenda in a structured table-like layout with time slots, topics, and presenter names aligned in columns. The HTML renders all items as plain paragraphs, losing the columnar structure. However, all text content is preserved.
